name: ETH Deep Reset Continuation v1
thesis: Even in mostly choppy ETH 4h conditions, directional bursts can emerge after a deeper reset into value. The edge should come from reset-and-resume structure with simple participation logic rather than shallow EMA-touch entries.
invalidation_condition: Abort if this collapses into zero-trade grammar, contradictory role assignments, or cannot show enough blind-window participation after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Continuation after a deeper pullback resets positioning but does not destroy structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Deeper pullbacks can improve reward/risk and flush weak hands before a short continuation burst resumes.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h offers enough structural persistence for reset-and-resume ideas while still being active enough to produce multiple setups.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the lane stays fully balance-dominated rather than transitional, continuation after reset may not follow through often enough.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If there is some participation but poor density, relax the structure relationship before altering trade management.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; structure, reset, and state qualifiers align
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: reduced by broad reset distance and modest ADX threshold
